message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
You are working on issue **flt-it8.4: GraphQL schema + resolvers** for the Flotsam project.

## Issue Description
Define GraphQL SDL (items, search, mutations) and implement gqlgen resolvers for Item CRUD and search.

## What You Must Build

### 1. Set up gqlgen — `api/gqlgen.yml`

Create the gqlgen config file. Use schema-first approach:
```yaml
schema:
  - graph/schema/*.graphql

exec:
  filename: graph/generated/generated.go
  package: generated

model:
  filename: graph/model/models_gen.go
  package: model

resolver:
  layout: follow-schema
  dir: graph/resolver
  package: resolver

autobind:
  - github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/graph/model

models:
  ID:
    model:
      - github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.ID
  DateTime:
    model:
      - github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Time
  JSON:
    model:
      - github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Map
```

Add `//go:generate go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate` to a `graph/generate.go` file.

### 2. GraphQL SDL — `api/graph/schema/`

Create SDL files defining the schema from the product spec:

**`api/graph/schema/schema.graphql`** — base schema with Query and Mutation types
**`api/graph/schema/item.graphql`** — Item type, enums, inputs, filters

Types to define (from product spec):
```graphql
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON

type Item {
  id: ID!
  type: ItemType!
  status: ItemStatus!
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel!
  title: String
  contentText: String
  contentUrl: String
  tags: [String!]!
  metadata: JSON!
  autoMetadata: JSON!
  source: String!
  sourceUrl: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  capturedAt: DateTime!
}

enum ItemType { BOOKMARK, VOICE_NOTE, TEXT_NOTE, DRIVE_DOC, HIGHLIGHT, FILE }
enum ItemStatus { PENDING, PROCESSING, READY, ERROR }
enum SensitivityLevel { PUBLIC, NORMAL, SENSITIVE, PRIVATE }

type SearchResult {
  items: [Item!]!
  total: Int!
  hasMore: Boolean!
}

input ItemFilter {
  types: [ItemType!]
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: [SensitivityLevel!]
  source: String
  dateFrom: DateTime
  dateTo: DateTime
  status: [ItemStatus!]
}

enum ItemSortField { CREATED_AT, UPDATED_AT, CAPTURED_AT, TITLE }
enum SortDirection { ASC, DESC }
input ItemSort {
  field: ItemSortField!
  direction: SortDirection!
}

input BookmarkInput {
  url: String!
  title: String
  selection: String
  referrer: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}

input NoteInput {
  title: String
  content: String!
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}

input UpdateItemInput {
  title: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}

type Query {
  item(id: ID!): Item
  items(filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int, offset: Int, sort: ItemSort): SearchResult!
  search(query: String!, filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int, threshold: Float): SearchResult!
}

type Mutation {
  captureBookmark(input: BookmarkInput!): Item!
  captureNote(input: NoteInput!): Item!
  updateItem(id: ID!, input: UpdateItemInput!): Item!
  deleteItem(id: ID!): Boolean!
  updateSensitivity(id: ID!, sensitivity: SensitivityLevel!): Item!
  tagItem(id: ID!, tags: [String!]!): Item!
}
```

### 3. `api/internal/item/` — Item service

Create the item service package that resolvers will call:

**`api/internal/item/service.go`**
```go
type Service struct {
    pool *pgxpool.Pool
}

func NewService(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *Service

func (s *Service) GetByID(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) List(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, filter *Filter, limit, offset int, sort *Sort) (*SearchResult, error)
func (s *Service) Search(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, query string, filter *Filter, limit int, threshold float64) (*SearchResult, error)
func (s *Service) CaptureBookmark(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, input BookmarkInput) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) CaptureNote(ctx context.Context, ownerID uuid.UUID, input NoteInput) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) Update(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, input UpdateInput) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) Delete(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID) error
func (s *Service) UpdateSensitivity(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, sensitivity string) (*Item, error)
func (s *Service) Tag(ctx context.Context, ownerID, itemID uuid.UUID, tags []string) (*Item, error)
```

**`api/internal/item/model.go`** — Go types for Item, Filter, Sort, SearchResult, inputs

**`api/internal/item/service_test.go`** — Unit tests using mock/stub DB (test input validation, business logic). Integration tests guarded by `testing.Short()`.

The service methods should use real pgx queries with parameterized SQL (`$1`, `$2`, etc.). For search, use the `search_vector` tsvector column with `ts_rank`. For semantic search, use pgvector `<=>` cosine distance (but the embedding parameter will be nil for now since we don't have the embedding provider yet — return an error if no embedding is available).

### 4. Resolver implementations — `api/graph/resolver/`

**`api/graph/resolver/resolver.go`**
```go
type Resolver struct {
    Items *item.Service
}
```

**`api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go`** — will be partially generated by gqlgen, you fill in the implementations:
- Each resolver extracts `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` to get the owner ID
- Mutations require auth (return error if claims are nil)
- Queries allow optional auth for now (but item access requires auth)
- Map between GraphQL model types and internal item types

### 5. Wire GraphQL into router

Modify `api/internal/router/router.go`:
- Replace the placeholder GraphQL handler with the real gqlgen handler
- Import `graph/generated` and `graph/resolver`
- Add `Items *item.Service` to `Deps` or construct it in `New()`
- Wire up the GraphQL Playground if `GQLPlayground` is enabled

### 6. Wire item service in `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`

Add `item.NewService(pool)` and pass to router deps.

### 7. Run code generation

After creating the SDL and gqlgen.yml:
```bash
go get github.com/99designs/gqlgen@latest
go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate
```

This generates `graph/generated/generated.go` and `graph/model/models_gen.go`. Then implement the resolver methods.

## Existing Code Context

**`api/internal/router/router.go`** — has Deps struct, placeholder handler for `/graphql`. Replace with real gqlgen handler.

**`api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`** — startup sequence. Add item service init and pass to router.

**`api/internal/auth/context.go`** — `GetClaims(ctx) *Claims` for extracting authenticated user.

**`api/internal/auth/auth.go`** — `Claims` struct with `UserID uuid.UUID`, `Email string`.

**Database schema** (from migration): items table with columns id, owner_id, type, status, sensitivity, title, content_text, content_url, embedding, search_vector, tags, metadata, auto_metadata, source, source_url, created_at, updated_at, captured_at. Enums: item_type, item_status, sensitivity_level.

## Quality Gate
Before reporting done, run:
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```
If golangci-lint is not installed, `go vet ./...` is sufficient.

## Important Notes
- **Never edit** generated files manually — run gqlgen generate
- All SQL must use parameterized queries (`$1`, `$2`)
- All item queries MUST filter by `owner_id` (multi-tenant security)
- Use `pgx` directly — no ORM

## Conventions
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("item: context: %w", err)` 
- Logging: structured slog
- Context: always first parameter
- SQL: always parameterized

## Commit Convention
```
Add GraphQL schema and item CRUD resolvers

Implements gqlgen schema-first GraphQL with Item types, search,
and mutation resolvers backed by pgx item service.

Issues: flt-it8.4
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

## WORKTREE ISOLATION — CRITICAL

### Create your worktree (MANDATORY FIRST STEP)
```bash
WORKTREE_NAME="graphql"
REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$WORKTREE_NAME"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/$WORKTREE_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi
cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```

Then verify:
```bash
echo "=== WORKTREE VERIFICATION ===" && \
echo "CWD: $(pwd)" && \
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)" && \
if echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/'; then \
  echo "✓ WORKTREE VERIFIED"; \
else \
  echo "✗ FATAL: NOT in a worktree! CWD=$(pwd)"; exit 1; \
fi
```

### Rules
1. NEVER cd to the main repo — stay in your worktree
2. NEVER run `git checkout` to switch branches
3. Before EVERY git commit, re-verify CWD is in `.claude/worktrees/`
4. Push with `git push origin HEAD`

</teammate-message>

   107→func parseCORSOrigins(s string) []string {

   109→	origins := make([]string, 0, len(parts))
   110→	for _, p := range parts {
   111→		p = strings.TrimSpace(p)
   112→		if p != "" {
   113→			origins = append(origins, p)
   114→		}
   115→	}
   116→	return origins
   117→}

    16→const maxConnectAttempts = 3
    17→
    18→// Connect creates a pgxpool.Pool, pings the database, and returns the pool.
    19→// It retries up to maxConnectAttempts times with exponential backoff.
    20→func Connect(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) (*pgxpool.Pool, error) {
    21→	cfg, err := pgxpool.ParseConfig(databaseURL)
    22→	if err != nil {
    23→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("db: failed to parse database URL: %w", err)
    24→	}
    25→
    26→	var pool *pgxpool.Pool
    27→	for attempt := 1; attempt <= maxConnectAttempts; attempt++ {
    28→		pool, err = pgxpool.NewWithConfig(ctx, cfg)
    29→		if err != nil {
    30→			slog.Warn("db: connect attempt failed", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
    31→			time.Sleep(time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second)
    32→			continue
    33→		}
    34→
    35→		if err = pool.Ping(ctx); err != nil {
    36→			pool.Close()
    37→			slog.Warn("db: ping attempt failed", "attempt", attempt, "error", err)
    38→			time.Sleep(time.Duration(attempt) * time.Second)
    39→			continue
    40→		}
    41→
    42→		stat := pool.Stat()
    43→		slog.Info("db: connected",
    44→			"attempt", attempt,
    45→			"max_conns", stat.MaxConns(),
    46→		)
    47→		return pool, nil
    48→	}
    49→
    50→	return nil, fmt.Errorf("db: failed to connect after %d attempts: %w", maxConnectAttempts, err)
    51→}

    56→	if err != nil {
    57→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("migrate: create provider: %w", err)
    58→	}
    59→	return provider, nil
    60→}

    62→// MigrateUp runs all pending goose UP migrations embedded in the binary.
    63→func MigrateUp(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) error {
    64→	slog.Info("migrate: running up migrations")
    65→	start := time.Now()
    66→
    67→	db, err := sql.Open("pgx", databaseURL)
    68→	if err != nil {
    69→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: failed to open db: %w", err)
    70→	}
    71→	defer db.Close() //nolint:errcheck // best-effort close
    72→
    73→	provider, err := newProvider(db)
    74→	if err != nil {
    75→		return err
    76→	}
    77→
    78→	results, err := provider.Up(ctx)
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		slog.Error("migrate: up failed", "error", err, "duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
    81→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: up: %w", err)
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	slog.Info("migrate: up completed",
    85→		"applied", len(results),
    86→		"duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds(),
    87→	)
    88→	return nil
    89→}

    91→// MigrateDown rolls back the most recent goose migration.
    92→func MigrateDown(ctx context.Context, databaseURL string) error {
    93→	slog.Info("migrate: rolling back last migration")
    94→	start := time.Now()
    95→
    96→	db, err := sql.Open("pgx", databaseURL)
    97→	if err != nil {
    98→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: failed to open db: %w", err)
    99→	}
   100→	defer db.Close() //nolint:errcheck // best-effort close
   101→
   102→	provider, err := newProvider(db)
   103→	if err != nil {
   104→		return err
   105→	}
   106→
   107→	result, err := provider.Down(ctx)
   108→	if err != nil {
   109→		slog.Error("migrate: down failed", "error", err, "duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
   110→		return fmt.Errorf("migrate: down: %w", err)
   111→	}
   112→
   113→	if result != nil {
   114→		slog.Info("migrate: down completed",
   115→			"rolled_back", result.Source.Path,
   116→			"duration_ms", time.Since(start).Milliseconds(),
   117→		)
   118→	} else {
   119→		slog.Info("migrate: no migrations to roll back")
   120→	}
   121→	return nil
   122→}

    10→
    11→// Auth validates the Bearer JWT token from the Authorization header.
    12→// On success, claims are stored in the request context via auth.WithClaims.
    13→// On failure, responds with 401 Unauthorized.
    14→func Auth(validator auth.Validator) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
    15→	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    16→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    17→			token, ok := bearerToken(r)
    18→			if !ok {
    19→				slog.WarnContext(r.Context(), "auth: missing or malformed authorization header",
    20→					"request_id", requestIDFromContext(r.Context()),
    21→					"method", r.Method,
    22→					"path", r.URL.Path,
    23→					"remote_addr", r.RemoteAddr,
    24→				)
    25→				w.Header().Set("WWW-Authenticate", `Bearer realm="flotsam"`)
    26→				http.Error(w, "missing or malformed authorization header", http.StatusUnauthorized)
    27→				return
    28→			}
    29→
    30→			claims, err := validator.Validate(token)
    31→			if err != nil {
    32→				slog.WarnContext(r.Context(), "auth: invalid or expired token",
    33→					"request_id", requestIDFromContext(r.Context()),
    34→					"method", r.Method,
    35→					"path", r.URL.Path,
    36→					"remote_addr", r.RemoteAddr,
    37→					"error", err,
    38→				)
    39→				w.Header().Set("WWW-Authenticate", `Bearer realm="flotsam", error="invalid_token"`)
    40→				http.Error(w, "invalid or expired token", http.StatusUnauthorized)
    41→				return
    42→			}
    43→
    44→			ctx := auth.WithClaims(r.Context(), claims)
    45→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    46→		})
    47→	}
    48→}
    49→
    50→// OptionalAuth validates a Bearer token if present but allows unauthenticated requests.
    51→// If a token is present and valid, claims are stored in context.
    52→// If a token is present but invalid, responds with 401.
    53→// If no token is present, the request proceeds without claims.
    54→func OptionalAuth(validator auth.Validator) func(http.Handler) http.Handler {
    55→	return func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    56→		return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    57→			token, ok := bearerToken(r)
    58→			if !ok {
    59→				next.ServeHTTP(w, r)
    60→				return
    61→			}
    62→
    63→			claims, err := validator.Validate(token)
    64→			if err != nil {
    65→				slog.WarnContext(r.Context(), "auth: invalid or expired token",
    66→					"request_id", requestIDFromContext(r.Context()),
    67→					"method", r.Method,
    68→					"path", r.URL.Path,
    69→					"remote_addr", r.RemoteAddr,
    70→					"error", err,
    71→				)
    72→				w.Header().Set("WWW-Authenticate", `Bearer realm="flotsam", error="invalid_token"`)
    73→				http.Error(w, "invalid or expired token", http.StatusUnauthorized)
    74→				return
    75→			}
    76→
    77→			ctx := auth.WithClaims(r.Context(), claims)
    78→			next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    79→		})
    80→	}
    81→}
    82→
    83→// bearerToken extracts the token from "Authorization: Bearer <token>".
    84→func bearerToken(r *http.Request) (string, bool) {
    85→	header := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
    86→	if header == "" {
    87→		return "", false
    88→	}
    89→	parts := strings.SplitN(header, " ", 2)
    90→	if len(parts) != 2 || !strings.EqualFold(parts[0], "bearer") {
    91→		return "", false
    92→	}
    93→	token := strings.TrimSpace(parts[1])
    94→	if token == "" {
    95→		return "", false
    96→	}
    97→	return token, true
    98→}

    28→// New constructs the chi router with all middleware, routes, and handlers.
    29→func New(deps Deps) http.Handler {
    30→	r := chi.NewRouter()
    31→
    32→	// Global middleware (applied to every request)
    33→	r.Use(flotsammiddleware.RequestID)
    34→	r.Use(flotsammiddleware.Logging)

    38→
    39→	// CORS (if configured)
    40→	if deps.Config.CORSOrigins != "" {
    41→		origins := parseCORSOrigins(deps.Config.CORSOrigins)
    42→		r.Use(cors.Handler(cors.Options{
    43→			AllowedOrigins:   origins,
    44→			AllowedMethods:   []string{"GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "OPTIONS"},
    45→			AllowedHeaders:   []string{"Accept", "Authorization", "Content-Type"},
    46→			AllowCredentials: true,

    50→
    51→	// Health endpoint — no auth
    52→	r.Get("/health", healthHandler)
    53→
    54→	// GraphQL endpoint — optional auth + rate limit
    55→	r.Route("/graphql", func(r chi.Router) {
    56→		r.Use(flotsammiddleware.OptionalAuth(deps.Validator))
    57→		if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {
    58→			rl := flotsammiddleware.NewRateLimiter(deps.Config.RateLimitGraphQL)
    59→			r.Use(rl.Limit)

    61→		r.Post("/", placeholderHandler("graphql"))
    62→		r.Get("/", placeholderHandler("graphql"))
    63→	})
    64→
    65→	// MCP endpoint — mandatory auth + rate limit
    66→	if deps.Config.MCPEnabled {
    67→		r.Route("/mcp", func(r chi.Router) {
    68→			r.Use(flotsammiddleware.Auth(deps.Validator))
    69→			if deps.Config.RateLimitEnabled {

    71→				r.Use(rl.Limit)

    77→
    78→	// Upload endpoint — mandatory auth

1→# Flotsam: Architecture Design & Product Overview
     2→
     3→## 1. Context & Vision
     4→
     5→**Flotsam** is a personal knowledge management system — a "second brain" for capturing thoughts, notes, URLs, and voice dictations, with semantic search and AI integration via MCP.
     6→
     7→**Core problems it solves:**
     8→- **Capture friction**: Too many clicks to save a thought, especially on mobile/while driving
     9→- **Retrieval**: Information is scattered across Google Drive, browser bookmarks, various apps — can't find things semantically
    10→- **AI integration**: AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, Cursor) can't access personal knowledge
    11→- **Data fragmentation**: No unified place for all types of personal information
    12→- **Data control**: User owns the data end-to-end, self-hosted on VPS
    13→
    14→**MVP scope**: Browser bookmarks (Chrome extension) + voice notes (Android phone app with voice trigger for driving)
    15→
    16→**Full vision** (post-MVP): Web app with query interface, Google Drive integration, iOS app, Firefox/Safari extensions, MCP with defense-in-depth authorization, content-level access control, multi-user support.
    17→
    18→**Inspiration**: Nate B. Jones' "Open Brain" (Supabase + Slack + MCP), Obsidian (graph view, backlinks, hierarchical tags, daily notes), Readwise (capture + highlights).
    19→

    70→## 3. Data Model
    71→
    72→### Core table: `items`
    73→
    74→Everything is an "item" — a unified type with JSONB metadata for type-specific fields.
    75→
    76→```sql
    77→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;
    78→CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;
    79→
    80→CREATE TYPE item_type AS ENUM (
    81→  'bookmark', 'voice_note', 'text_note', 'drive_doc', 'highlight', 'file'
    82→);
    83→
    84→CREATE TYPE item_status AS ENUM (
    85→  'pending',      -- just captured, processing not complete
    86→  'processing',   -- async processing in progress
    87→  'ready',        -- fully processed, searchable
    88→  'error'         -- processing failed
    89→);
    90→
    91→CREATE TYPE sensitivity_level AS ENUM (
    92→  'public',       -- shareable, external APIs ok
    93→  'normal',       -- default, external APIs ok
    94→  'sensitive',    -- use local models, restricted MCP access
    95→  'private'       -- local models only, no MCP access
    96→);
    97→
    98→CREATE TABLE items (
    99→  id            UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   100→  owner_id      UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
   101→  type          item_type NOT NULL,
   102→  status        item_status NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
   103→  sensitivity   sensitivity_level NOT NULL DEFAULT 'normal',
   104→
   105→  -- Content
   106→  title         TEXT,
   107→  content_text  TEXT,                    -- searchable text (page extract, transcription, note body)
   108→  content_url   TEXT,                    -- S3 key for large content (HTML, audio, images)
   109→
   110→  -- Search
   111→  embedding     vector(1536),            -- semantic search vector
   112→  search_vector tsvector GENERATED ALWAYS AS (
   113→    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(title, '')), 'A') ||
   114→    setweight(to_tsvector('english', coalesce(content_text, '')), 'B')
   115→  ) STORED,
   116→
   117→  -- Organization
   118→  tags          TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',     -- hierarchical tags: 'project/flotsam', 'person/sarah'
   119→  metadata      JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',  -- type-specific fields
   120→  auto_metadata JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',  -- LLM-extracted classification
   121→
   122→  -- Provenance
   123→  source        TEXT NOT NULL,           -- 'chrome_extension', 'android_app', 'web_app', 'mcp', 'api'
   124→  source_url    TEXT,                    -- original URL for bookmarks, referrer, etc.
   125→
   126→  -- Timestamps
   127→  created_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
   128→  updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
   129→  captured_at   TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()  -- when user actually captured (may differ from created_at)
   130→);
   131→
   132→-- Indexes
   133→CREATE INDEX idx_items_owner ON items(owner_id);
   134→CREATE INDEX idx_items_type ON items(owner_id, type);
   135→CREATE INDEX idx_items_created ON items(owner_id, created_at DESC);
   136→CREATE INDEX idx_items_tags ON items USING GIN(tags);
   137→CREATE INDEX idx_items_metadata ON items USING GIN(metadata);
   138→CREATE INDEX idx_items_search ON items USING GIN(search_vector);
   139→CREATE INDEX idx_items_embedding ON items USING ivfflat(embedding vector_cosine_ops) WITH (lists = 100);
   140→CREATE INDEX idx_items_sensitivity ON items(owner_id, sensitivity);
   141→```

   234→

   313→
   314→## 5. Auth & Authorization Architecture
   315→
   316→### Authentication flow
   317→
   318→1. **Web app**: Email/password login → JWT (access + refresh tokens)
   319→2. **Chrome extension**: User logs in via web app, extension gets token via `chrome.identity` or manual paste
   320→3. **Android app**: Email/password → JWT
   321→4. **MCP clients**: Per-client API keys (registered in web UI)
   322→5. **API direct**: Bearer JWT or API key
   323→
   324→### JWT claims
   325→
   326→```go
   327→type Claims struct {
   328→    jwt.RegisteredClaims
   329→    UserID     uuid.UUID `json:"uid"`
   330→    Email      string    `json:"email"`
   331→    ClientType string    `json:"ctype"` // "web", "extension", "mobile", "api"
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→### Authorization model
   336→
   337→Multi-tenant from day 1:
   338→- Every item has `owner_id`
   339→- All queries filter by authenticated user's ID
   340→- Row-level security in PostgreSQL as defense-in-depth
   341→- RBAC columns in users table for future multi-user scenarios
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## 6. MCP Authorization Model
   346→
   347→Defense-in-depth with three layers:
   348→
   349→### Layer 1: Per-client tool permissions
   350→
   351→Each MCP client has an allowlist of tools and maximum sensitivity level:
   352→```json
   353→{
   354→  "tools": ["search", "browse", "capture"],
   355→  "max_sensitivity": "normal",
   356→  "read_only": false
   357→}
   358→```
   359→
   360→### Layer 2: Human-in-the-loop approval
   361→
   362→Certain operations require real-time user approval:
   363→- Configured per-client in `requires_approval` array
   364→- When triggered: request is held, push notification sent to user's device
   365→- User approves/denies via web app or mobile app
   366→- Timeout after configurable period (default: 5 minutes) → deny
   367→
   368→### Layer 3: Content-level filtering
   369→
   370→- Items with `sensitivity = 'private'` are never returned via MCP
   371→- Items with `sensitivity = 'sensitive'` only returned to clients with `max_sensitivity >= 'sensitive'`
   372→- Auto-classification suggests sensitivity on capture; user can override
   373→- Audit log records every MCP access for accountability
   374→
   375→---
   376→
   377→## 7. Content Processing Pipeline
   378→
   379→### Sync operations (block API response)
   380→- Text embedding generation (< 1s for short content)
   381→- Tag extraction from metadata
   382→- Input validation
   383→
   384→### Async operations (background jobs)
   385→- Page content fetching for bookmarks (fetch URL, extract readable content)
   386→- Audio transcription (Whisper API or local)
   387→- Auto-metadata extraction (LLM classification)
   388→- Sensitivity auto-classification
   389→- Large content upload to S3
   390→- Re-embedding after content changes
   391→
   392→### Job processing
   393→
   394→PostgreSQL-backed job queue (River):
   395→- Jobs stored in PG (no new infrastructure)
   396→- Retries with exponential backoff
   397→- Dead letter queue for failed jobs
   398→- Status tracking (items move from `pending` → `processing` → `ready` / `error`)
   399→
   400→---
   401→
   402→## 8. Storage Architecture
   403→
   404→### PostgreSQL (structured data + search)
   405→- Item metadata, user data, MCP client registry, audit log
   406→- pgvector embeddings for semantic search
   407→- Full-text search indexes
   408→- Background job queue (River)
   409→- Managed via goose migrations
   410→
   411→### S3-Compatible Object Storage (large content)
   412→- Development: MinIO in Docker
   413→- Production: Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible, no egress fees)
   414→- Stores: page HTML/content, audio files, images, document snapshots
   415→- Items reference S3 objects via `content_url` column
   416→
   417→### Key hierarchy
   418→```
   419→s3://flotsam-{env}/
   420→  {owner_id}/
   421→    bookmarks/{item_id}/content.html
   422→    voice_notes/{item_id}/audio.webm
   423→    voice_notes/{item_id}/audio.mp3
   424→    files/{item_id}/{filename}
   425→```
   426→
   427→---
   428→
   429→## 9. Provider Abstraction
   430→
   431→Pluggable interfaces for AI services, with local fallback for sensitive content:
   432→
   433→```go
   434→type EmbeddingProvider interface {
   435→    Embed(ctx context.Context, text string) ([]float32, error)
   436→    EmbedBatch(ctx context.Context, texts []string) ([][]float32, error)
   437→    Dimensions() int
   438→}
   439→
   440→type TranscriptionProvider interface {
   441→    Transcribe(ctx context.Context, audio io.Reader, opts TranscribeOpts) (string, error)
   442→}
   443→
   444→type ClassificationProvider interface {
   445→    Classify(ctx context.Context, content string) (*Classification, error)
   446→}
   447→```
   448→
   449→**Implementations:**
   450→| Provider | Embedding | Transcription | Classification |
   451→|----------|-----------|---------------|----------------|
   452→| OpenAI | text-embedding-3-small (1536d) | Whisper API | gpt-4o-mini |
   453→| Local | nomic-embed-text via Ollama | whisper.cpp | llama via Ollama |
   454→
   455→**Routing logic**: If item sensitivity >= `sensitive`, use local providers. Otherwise, use configured default (OpenAI).
   456→
   457→---
   458→
   459→## 10. Ideas Stolen from Obsidian
   460→
   461→- **Hierarchical tags**: `project/flotsam`, `person/sarah`, `topic/career` — not flat tags
   462→- **Backlinks**: When a bookmark references another captured URL, create a link. Surface "what links to this" in the UI.
   463→- **Daily digest**: Auto-generated summary of items captured each day
   464→- **Graph view** (future): Visualize connections between items based on shared tags, backlinks, semantic similarity
   465→- **Quick capture**: Obsidian's "quick add" pattern — minimal friction to save a thought

I now have all the patterns I need. Let me compose the implementation plan.

Here is the detailed implementation plan for flt-it8.4: GraphQL schema + resolvers.

---

## Implementation Plan: GraphQL Schema + Resolvers (flt-it8.4)

### Overview

Add gqlgen (schema-first) GraphQL support to the Flotsam API. This includes the SDL schema files, generated code, the `internal/item` service package with CRUD + search, resolver implementations, router wiring, custom scalars, and unit tests. Follows the exact patterns established in the kapow reference project.

---

### Step 1: Add gqlgen dependency

Run `go get github.com/99designs/gqlgen@latest` in `api/`. This adds the gqlgen library plus its handler/transport/extension/playground packages to go.mod.

---

### Step 2: Create `api/graph/generate.go`

Single file with the `go:generate` directive, identical to kapow:

```go
package graph

//go:generate go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate
```

---

### Step 3: Create `api/gqlgen.yml`

Configuration file at `api/gqlgen.yml`:

```yaml
schema:
  - graph/schema/*.graphql

exec:
  filename: graph/generated/generated.go
  package: generated

model:
  filename: graph/model/models_gen.go
  package: model

resolver:
  layout: follow-schema
  dir: graph/resolver
  package: resolver
  filename_template: "{name}.resolvers.go"

autobind: []

models:
  JSON:
    model: github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Map
  DateTime:
    model: github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Time
```

Key decisions:
- Use `graphql.Map` for the JSON scalar (JSONB metadata fields) -- gqlgen ships this built-in, maps to `map[string]interface{}`
- Use `graphql.Time` for DateTime -- handles RFC3339 serialization automatically
- No custom models mapped yet; let gqlgen generate enum types into `models_gen.go`

---

### Step 4: Create GraphQL SDL files in `api/graph/schema/`

Split across multiple files for clarity:

**`api/graph/schema/scalars.graphql`**
```graphql
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON
```

**`api/graph/schema/enums.graphql`**
```graphql
enum ItemType {
  BOOKMARK
  VOICE_NOTE
  TEXT_NOTE
  DRIVE_DOC
  HIGHLIGHT
  FILE
}

enum ItemStatus {
  PENDING
  PROCESSING
  READY
  ERROR
}

enum SensitivityLevel {
  PUBLIC
  NORMAL
  SENSITIVE
  PRIVATE
}

enum ItemSortField {
  CREATED_AT
  UPDATED_AT
  CAPTURED_AT
  TITLE
}

enum SortDirection {
  ASC
  DESC
}
```

**`api/graph/schema/item.graphql`**
```graphql
type Item {
  id: ID!
  type: ItemType!
  status: ItemStatus!
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel!
  title: String
  contentText: String
  contentUrl: String
  tags: [String!]!
  metadata: JSON!
  autoMetadata: JSON!
  source: String!
  sourceUrl: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  capturedAt: DateTime!
}

type ItemConnection {
  items: [Item!]!
  total: Int!
  hasMore: Boolean!
}

input ItemFilter {
  types: [ItemType!]
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: [SensitivityLevel!]
  source: String
  dateFrom: DateTime
  dateTo: DateTime
  status: [ItemStatus!]
}

input ItemSort {
  field: ItemSortField!
  direction: SortDirection
}

input BookmarkInput {
  url: String!
  title: String
  selection: String
  referrer: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
  source: String
}

input NoteInput {
  title: String
  body: String!
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
  source: String
}

input UpdateItemInput {
  title: String
  contentText: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}
```

**`api/graph/schema/schema.graphql`**
```graphql
type Query {
  item(id: ID!): Item
  items(filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int, offset: Int, sort: ItemSort): ItemConnection!
  search(query: String!, filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int): ItemConnection!
}

type Mutation {
  captureBookmark(input: BookmarkInput!): Item!
  captureNote(input: NoteInput!): Item!
  updateItem(id: ID!, input: UpdateItemInput!): Item!
  deleteItem(id: ID!): Boolean!
  tagItem(id: ID!, tags: [String!]!): Item!
}
```

Design notes:
- `me` query deferred to flt-it8.5 (Auth issue)
- `updateSensitivity` folded into `updateItem` for simplicity
- `ItemConnection` renamed from `SearchResult` for clarity (same shape)
- `source` is optional in `BookmarkInput`/`NoteInput` -- defaults to `"api"` in the service layer
- `search` is FTS-only for now; semantic search added later when embedding provider is wired

---

### Step 5: Run `make api-generate`

This creates:
- `api/graph/generated/generated.go` (executable schema)
- `api/graph/model/models_gen.go` (generated Go types for all SDL types/inputs/enums)

Both are gitignored-by-convention (never hand-edit). The resolver stubs will be generated into `api/graph/resolver/`.

---

### Step 6: Create `api/internal/item/model.go` -- Domain types

Internal Go types that map to database columns. Resolvers translate between these and gqlgen-generated types.

```go
package item

type Item struct {
    ID           uuid.UUID
    OwnerID      uuid.UUID
    Type         string     // "bookmark", "voice_note", etc.
    Status       string     // "pending", "processing", "ready", "error"
    Sensitivity  string     // "public", "normal", "sensitive", "private"
    Title        *string
    ContentText  *string
    ContentURL   *string
    Tags         []string
    Metadata     map[string]any
    AutoMetadata map[string]any
    Source       string
    SourceURL    *string
    CreatedAt    time.Time
    UpdatedAt    time.Time
    CapturedAt   time.Time
}

type ListParams struct {
    OwnerID     uuid.UUID
    Filter      *Filter
    Limit       int
    Offset      int
    SortField   string
    SortDir     string
}

type Filter struct {
    Types       []string
    Tags        []string
    Sensitivity []string
    Source      *string
    DateFrom   *time.Time
    DateTo     *time.Time
    Status     []string
}

type SearchParams struct {
    OwnerID uuid.UUID
    Query   string
    Filter  *Filter
    Limit   int
}
```

---

### Step 7: Create `api/internal/item/service.go` -- CRUD + search

The `Service` struct holds a `*pgxpool.Pool` and exposes methods. All queries filter by `owner_id`.

```go
package item

type Service struct {
    pool *pgxpool.Pool
}

func New(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *Service {
    return &Service{pool: pool}
}
```

Methods:

- **`Get(ctx, ownerID, itemID) (*Item, error)`** -- SELECT by id + owner_id. Returns `nil, nil` if not found.
- **`List(ctx, params ListParams) ([]*Item, int, error)`** -- Dynamic WHERE clause built from filter params. Returns items + total count. Limit defaults to 20, max 100.
- **`Search(ctx, params SearchParams) ([]*Item, int, error)`** -- Uses `search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $query)` with `ts_rank` ordering. Same filter support as List.
- **`CreateBookmark(ctx, ownerID, input) (*Item, error)`** -- INSERT with type=bookmark, status=pending, source default "api".
- **`CreateNote(ctx, ownerID, input) (*Item, error)`** -- INSERT with type=text_note, status=ready (notes are immediately searchable).
- **`Update(ctx, ownerID, itemID, input) (*Item, error)`** -- Partial UPDATE (only non-nil fields). Returns updated item.
- **`Delete(ctx, ownerID, itemID) error`** -- DELETE by id + owner_id. Returns error if not found.
- **`SetTags(ctx, ownerID, itemID, tags) (*Item, error)`** -- UPDATE tags array. Returns updated item.

SQL patterns:
- All queries use `$N` placeholders, never `fmt.Sprintf`
- All queries include `WHERE owner_id = $1` as the first condition
- Dynamic filters built with a query builder helper (append conditions + args)
- `RETURNING` clause on INSERT/UPDATE to avoid a second round-trip
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("item: get: %w", err)`

Query builder helper (unexported):
```go
type queryBuilder struct {
    conditions []string
    args       []any
}

func (qb *queryBuilder) add(condition string, arg any) {
    qb.args = append(qb.args, arg)
    qb.conditions = append(qb.conditions, fmt.Sprintf(condition, len(qb.args)))
}
```

---

### Step 8: Create `api/internal/item/scan.go` -- Row scanning helper

Shared function to scan a `pgx.Row` or `pgx.Rows` into an `*Item`. Avoids duplication across Get/List/Search/Create/Update.

```go
func scanItem(row pgx.Row) (*Item, error) {
    var it Item
    err := row.Scan(
        &it.ID, &it.OwnerID, &it.Type, &it.Status, &it.Sensitivity,
        &it.Title, &it.ContentText, &it.ContentURL,
        &it.Tags, &it.Metadata, &it.AutoMetadata,
        &it.Source, &it.SourceURL,
        &it.CreatedAt, &it.UpdatedAt, &it.CapturedAt,
    )
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    return &it, nil
}
```

Note: pgx handles `text[]` -> `[]string`, `jsonb` -> `map[string]any`, `timestamptz` -> `time.Time` natively.

---

### Step 9: Create `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` -- Dependency root

```go
package resolver

import "github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/item"

type Resolver struct {
    Items *item.Service
}
```

Following kapow's pattern exactly: flat struct, injected by router.

---

### Step 10: Implement resolvers in `api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go`

After gqlgen generates stubs, fill in each resolver. Key patterns:

**Auth check helper** (used in every resolver):
```go
func requireAuth(ctx context.Context) (*auth.Claims, error) {
    claims := auth.GetClaims(ctx)
    if claims == nil {
        return nil, errors.New("resolver: authentication required")
    }
    return claims, nil
}
```

**Enum mapping helpers** (convert between gqlgen enums and DB strings):
```go
func itemTypeToString(t model.ItemType) string {
    return strings.ToLower(string(t))  // BOOKMARK -> bookmark
}

func stringToItemType(s string) model.ItemType {
    return model.ItemType(strings.ToUpper(s))  // bookmark -> BOOKMARK
}
```

Same pattern for `ItemStatus`, `SensitivityLevel`.

**Item domain-to-model mapper**:
```go
func toGraphQLItem(it *item.Item) *model.Item {
    return &model.Item{
        ID:           it.ID.String(),
        Type:         stringToItemType(it.Type),
        Status:       stringToItemStatus(it.Status),
        Sensitivity:  stringToSensitivity(it.Sensitivity),
        Title:        it.Title,
        ContentText:  it.ContentText,
        ContentURL:   it.ContentURL,
        Tags:         it.Tags,
        Metadata:     it.Metadata,
        AutoMetadata: it.AutoMetadata,
        Source:       it.Source,
        SourceURL:    it.SourceURL,
        CreatedAt:    it.CreatedAt,
        UpdatedAt:    it.UpdatedAt,
        CapturedAt:   it.CapturedAt,
    }
}
```

**Example resolver -- `item` query**:
```go
func (r *queryResolver) Item(ctx context.Context, id string) (*model.Item, error) {
    claims, err := requireAuth(ctx)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    itemID, err := uuid.Parse(id)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolver: invalid item id: %w", err)
    }
    it, err := r.Items.Get(ctx, claims.UserID, itemID)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolver: item: %w", err)
    }
    if it == nil {
        return nil, nil
    }

}
```

**Example resolver -- `captureBookmark` mutation**:
```go
func (r *mutationResolver) CaptureBookmark(ctx context.Context, input model.BookmarkInput) (*model.Item, error) {
    claims, err := requireAuth(ctx)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    // Validate URL
    if _, err := url.ParseRequestURI(input.URL); err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolver: invalid url: %w", err)
    }
    it, err := r.Items.CreateBookmark(ctx, claims.UserID, item.BookmarkParams{
        URL:         input.URL,
        Title:       input.Title,
        Selection:   input.Selection,
        Referrer:    input.Referrer,
        Tags:        input.Tags,
        Sensitivity: sensitivityToStringOrDefault(input.Sensitivity, "normal"),
        Source:      stringOrDefault(input.Source, "api"),
    })
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolver: capture bookmark: %w", err)
    }
    return toGraphQLItem(it), nil
}
```

```go
gqlSrv.AddTransport(transport.POST{})
gqlSrv.AddTransport(transport.GET{})
```

```go
    r.Get("/playground", playground.Handler("Flotsam API", "/graphql"))
}
```

### Risks and mitigations

1. **pgx type scanning for `text[]` and `jsonb`**: These work natively with pgx v5, but must be verified against a real DB in integration tests (deferred to flt-it8.19 testcontainer work).

Here is the detailed implementation plan for flt-it8.4: GraphQL schema + resolvers.

---

## Implementation Plan: GraphQL Schema + Resolvers (flt-it8.4)

### Overview

Add gqlgen (schema-first) GraphQL support to the Flotsam API. This includes the SDL schema files, generated code, the `internal/item` service package with CRUD + search, resolver implementations, router wiring, custom scalars, and unit tests. Follows the exact patterns established in the kapow reference project.

---

### Step 1: Add gqlgen dependency

Run `go get github.com/99designs/gqlgen@latest` in `api/`. This adds the gqlgen library plus its handler/transport/extension/playground packages to go.mod.

---

### Step 2: Create `api/graph/generate.go`

Single file with the `go:generate` directive, identical to kapow:

```go
package graph

//go:generate go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate
```

---

### Step 3: Create `api/gqlgen.yml`

Configuration file at `api/gqlgen.yml`:

```yaml
schema:
  - graph/schema/*.graphql

exec:
  filename: graph/generated/generated.go
  package: generated

model:
  filename: graph/model/models_gen.go
  package: model

resolver:
  layout: follow-schema
  dir: graph/resolver
  package: resolver
  filename_template: "{name}.resolvers.go"

autobind: []

models:
  JSON:
    model: github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Map
  DateTime:
    model: github.com/99designs/gqlgen/graphql.Time
```

Key decisions:
- Use `graphql.Map` for the JSON scalar (JSONB metadata fields) -- gqlgen ships this built-in, maps to `map[string]interface{}`
- Use `graphql.Time` for DateTime -- handles RFC3339 serialization automatically
- No custom models mapped yet; let gqlgen generate enum types into `models_gen.go`

---

### Step 4: Create GraphQL SDL files in `api/graph/schema/`

Split across multiple files for clarity:

**`api/graph/schema/scalars.graphql`**
```graphql
scalar DateTime
scalar JSON
```

**`api/graph/schema/enums.graphql`**
```graphql
enum ItemType {
  BOOKMARK
  VOICE_NOTE
  TEXT_NOTE
  DRIVE_DOC
  HIGHLIGHT
  FILE
}

enum ItemStatus {
  PENDING
  PROCESSING
  READY
  ERROR
}

enum SensitivityLevel {
  PUBLIC
  NORMAL
  SENSITIVE
  PRIVATE
}

enum ItemSortField {
  CREATED_AT
  UPDATED_AT
  CAPTURED_AT
  TITLE
}

enum SortDirection {
  ASC
  DESC
}
```

**`api/graph/schema/item.graphql`**
```graphql
type Item {
  id: ID!
  type: ItemType!
  status: ItemStatus!
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel!
  title: String
  contentText: String
  contentUrl: String
  tags: [String!]!
  metadata: JSON!
  autoMetadata: JSON!
  source: String!
  sourceUrl: String
  createdAt: DateTime!
  updatedAt: DateTime!
  capturedAt: DateTime!
}

type ItemConnection {
  items: [Item!]!
  total: Int!
  hasMore: Boolean!
}

input ItemFilter {
  types: [ItemType!]
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: [SensitivityLevel!]
  source: String
  dateFrom: DateTime
  dateTo: DateTime
  status: [ItemStatus!]
}

input ItemSort {
  field: ItemSortField!
  direction: SortDirection
}

input BookmarkInput {
  url: String!
  title: String
  selection: String
  referrer: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
  source: String
}

input NoteInput {
  title: String
  body: String!
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
  source: String
}

input UpdateItemInput {
  title: String
  contentText: String
  tags: [String!]
  sensitivity: SensitivityLevel
}
```

**`api/graph/schema/schema.graphql`**
```graphql
type Query {
  item(id: ID!): Item
  items(filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int, offset: Int, sort: ItemSort): ItemConnection!
  search(query: String!, filter: ItemFilter, limit: Int): ItemConnection!
}

type Mutation {
  captureBookmark(input: BookmarkInput!): Item!
  captureNote(input: NoteInput!): Item!
  updateItem(id: ID!, input: UpdateItemInput!): Item!
  deleteItem(id: ID!): Boolean!
  tagItem(id: ID!, tags: [String!]!): Item!
}
```

Design notes:
- `me` query deferred to flt-it8.5 (Auth issue)
- `updateSensitivity` folded into `updateItem` for simplicity
- `ItemConnection` renamed from `SearchResult` for clarity (same shape)
- `source` is optional in `BookmarkInput`/`NoteInput` -- defaults to `"api"` in the service layer
- `search` is FTS-only for now; semantic search added later when embedding provider is wired

---

### Step 5: Run `make api-generate`

This creates:
- `api/graph/generated/generated.go` (executable schema)
- `api/graph/model/models_gen.go` (generated Go types for all SDL types/inputs/enums)

Both are gitignored-by-convention (never hand-edit). The resolver stubs will be generated into `api/graph/resolver/`.

---

### Step 6: Create `api/internal/item/model.go` -- Domain types

Internal Go types that map to database columns. Resolvers translate between these and gqlgen-generated types.

```go
package item

type Item struct {
    ID           uuid.UUID
    OwnerID      uuid.UUID
    Type         string     // "bookmark", "voice_note", etc.
    Status       string     // "pending", "processing", "ready", "error"
    Sensitivity  string     // "public", "normal", "sensitive", "private"
    Title        *string
    ContentText  *string
    ContentURL   *string
    Tags         []string
    Metadata     map[string]any
    AutoMetadata map[string]any
    Source       string
    SourceURL    *string
    CreatedAt    time.Time
    UpdatedAt    time.Time
    CapturedAt   time.Time
}

type ListParams struct {
    OwnerID     uuid.UUID
    Filter      *Filter
    Limit       int
    Offset      int
    SortField   string
    SortDir     string
}

type Filter struct {
    Types       []string
    Tags        []string
    Sensitivity []string
    Source      *string
    DateFrom   *time.Time
    DateTo     *time.Time
    Status     []string
}

type SearchParams struct {
    OwnerID uuid.UUID
    Query   string
    Filter  *Filter
    Limit   int
}
```

---

### Step 7: Create `api/internal/item/service.go` -- CRUD + search

The `Service` struct holds a `*pgxpool.Pool` and exposes methods. All queries filter by `owner_id`.

```go
package item

type Service struct {
    pool *pgxpool.Pool
}

func New(pool *pgxpool.Pool) *Service {
    return &Service{pool: pool}
}
```

Methods:

- **`Get(ctx, ownerID, itemID) (*Item, error)`** -- SELECT by id + owner_id. Returns `nil, nil` if not found.
- **`List(ctx, params ListParams) ([]*Item, int, error)`** -- Dynamic WHERE clause built from filter params. Returns items + total count. Limit defaults to 20, max 100.
- **`Search(ctx, params SearchParams) ([]*Item, int, error)`** -- Uses `search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery('english', $query)` with `ts_rank` ordering. Same filter support as List.
- **`CreateBookmark(ctx, ownerID, input) (*Item, error)`** -- INSERT with type=bookmark, status=pending, source default "api".
- **`CreateNote(ctx, ownerID, input) (*Item, error)`** -- INSERT with type=text_note, status=ready (notes are immediately searchable).
- **`Update(ctx, ownerID, itemID, input) (*Item, error)`** -- Partial UPDATE (only non-nil fields). Returns updated item.
- **`Delete(ctx, ownerID, itemID) error`** -- DELETE by id + owner_id. Returns error if not found.
- **`SetTags(ctx, ownerID, itemID, tags) (*Item, error)`** -- UPDATE tags array. Returns updated item.

SQL patterns:
- All queries use `$N` placeholders, never `fmt.Sprintf`
- All queries include `WHERE owner_id = $1` as the first condition
- Dynamic filters built with a query builder helper (append conditions + args)
- `RETURNING` clause on INSERT/UPDATE to avoid a second round-trip
- Error wrapping: `fmt.Errorf("item: get: %w", err)`

Query builder helper (unexported):
```go
type queryBuilder struct {
    conditions []string
    args       []any
}

func (qb *queryBuilder) add(condition string, arg any) {
    qb.args = append(qb.args, arg)
    qb.conditions = append(qb.conditions, fmt.Sprintf(condition, len(qb.args)))
}
```

---

### Step 8: Create `api/internal/item/scan.go` -- Row scanning helper

Shared function to scan a `pgx.Row` or `pgx.Rows` into an `*Item`. Avoids duplication across Get/List/Search/Create/Update.

```go
func scanItem(row pgx.Row) (*Item, error) {
    var it Item
    err := row.Scan(
        &it.ID, &it.OwnerID, &it.Type, &it.Status, &it.Sensitivity,
        &it.Title, &it.ContentText, &it.ContentURL,
        &it.Tags, &it.Metadata, &it.AutoMetadata,
        &it.Source, &it.SourceURL,
        &it.CreatedAt, &it.UpdatedAt, &it.CapturedAt,
    )
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    return &it, nil
}
```

Note: pgx handles `text[]` -> `[]string`, `jsonb` -> `map[string]any`, `timestamptz` -> `time.Time` natively.

---

### Step 9: Create `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` -- Dependency root

```go
package resolver

import "github.com/ketang/flotsam/api/internal/item"

type Resolver struct {
    Items *item.Service
}
```

Following kapow's pattern exactly: flat struct, injected by router.

---

### Step 10: Implement resolvers in `api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go`

After gqlgen generates stubs, fill in each resolver. Key patterns:

**Auth check helper** (used in every resolver):
```go
func requireAuth(ctx context.Context) (*auth.Claims, error) {
    claims := auth.GetClaims(ctx)
    if claims == nil {
        return nil, errors.New("resolver: authentication required")
    }
    return claims, nil
}
```

**Enum mapping helpers** (convert between gqlgen enums and DB strings):
```go
func itemTypeToString(t model.ItemType) string {
    return strings.ToLower(string(t))  // BOOKMARK -> bookmark
}

func stringToItemType(s string) model.ItemType {
    return model.ItemType(strings.ToUpper(s))  // bookmark -> BOOKMARK
}
```

Same pattern for `ItemStatus`, `SensitivityLevel`.

**Item domain-to-model mapper**:
```go
func toGraphQLItem(it *item.Item) *model.Item {
    return &model.Item{
        ID:           it.ID.String(),
        Type:         stringToItemType(it.Type),
        Status:       stringToItemStatus(it.Status),
        Sensitivity:  stringToSensitivity(it.Sensitivity),
        Title:        it.Title,
        ContentText:  it.ContentText,
        ContentURL:   it.ContentURL,
        Tags:         it.Tags,
        Metadata:     it.Metadata,
        AutoMetadata: it.AutoMetadata,
        Source:       it.Source,
        SourceURL:    it.SourceURL,
        CreatedAt:    it.CreatedAt,
        UpdatedAt:    it.UpdatedAt,
        CapturedAt:   it.CapturedAt,
    }
}
```

**Example resolver -- `item` query**:
```go
func (r *queryResolver) Item(ctx context.Context, id string) (*model.Item, error) {
    claims, err := requireAuth(ctx)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    itemID, err := uuid.Parse(id)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolver: invalid item id: %w", err)
    }
    it, err := r.Items.Get(ctx, claims.UserID, itemID)
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolver: item: %w", err)
    }
    if it == nil {
        return nil, nil
    }
    return toGraphQLItem(it), nil
}
```

**Example resolver -- `captureBookmark` mutation**:
```go
func (r *mutationResolver) CaptureBookmark(ctx context.Context, input model.BookmarkInput) (*model.Item, error) {
    claims, err := requireAuth(ctx)

        return nil, err

        Referrer:    input.Referrer,
        Tags:        input.Tags,
        Sensitivity: sensitivityToStringOrDefault(input.Sensitivity, "normal"),
        Source:      stringOrDefault(input.Source, "api"),
    })
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("resolver: capture bookmark: %w", err)
    }
    return toGraphQLItem(it), nil
}
```

---

### Step 11: Wire into router (`api/internal/router/router.go`)

Replace the placeholder handler with the real gqlgen server. Changes to `router.go`:

2. Build gqlgen server:

**`api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers_test.go`**

- `toGraphQLItem` correctly maps all fields including enum conversions
- `items` query with nil filter/sort works (defaults)
- `deleteItem` returns true on success

---

### Step 15: Verify

1. `make api-generate` succeeds (gqlgen codegen)
2. `make api-build` compiles all three binaries
3. `make api-test-unit` passes with >= 80% coverage on `internal/item`
4. `make api-lint` passes
5. Manual smoke test: start server, POST a `captureBookmark` mutation via curl, query it back

---

| File | Action | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `api/go.mod` | modify | Add gqlgen dependency |
| `api/gqlgen.yml` | create | gqlgen configuration |
| `api/graph/generate.go` | create | go:generate directive |
| `api/graph/schema/scalars.graphql` | create | DateTime, JSON scalars |
| `api/graph/schema/enums.graphql` | create | ItemType, ItemStatus, SensitivityLevel, sorts |
| `api/graph/schema/item.graphql` | create | Item type, inputs, ItemConnection |
| `api/graph/schema/schema.graphql` | create | Query + Mutation root types |
| `api/graph/generated/generated.go` | generated | gqlgen executable schema |
| `api/graph/model/models_gen.go` | generated | gqlgen model types |
| `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` | create | Resolver struct with deps |
| `api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go` | create (then edit) | Resolver implementations |
| `api/graph/resolver/helpers.go` | create | Auth check, enum mappers, model mapper |
| `api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers_test.go` | create | Resolver unit tests |
| `api/internal/item/model.go` | create | Domain types (Item, ListParams, Filter, etc.) |

| `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go` | modify | Create item.Service, pass to router |

---

### Risks and mitigations

1. **pgx type scanning for `text[]` and `jsonb`**: These work natively with pgx v5, but must be verified against a real DB in integration tests (deferred to flt-it8.19 testcontainer work).

2. **Generated code size**: `generated.go` can be large. Add it to `.gitignore` only if the team prefers -- currently kapow commits it, so follow that convention.

3. **Search query injection via filter values**: All filters use parameterized queries. The query builder must never interpolate user input into SQL strings.

4. **Enum drift between SDL and DB**: The GraphQL enums (UPPER_CASE) must map 1:1 to Postgres enums (lower_snake_case). The string conversion helpers handle this. If a new enum value is added to the DB migration, it must also be added to the SDL.

# Plan: GraphQL Schema + Resolvers (flt-it8.4)

## Context

The Flotsam API has a working HTTP server with auth middleware, database connection, and a placeholder `/graphql` endpoint returning 501. This plan adds the real GraphQL layer: SDL schema, gqlgen codegen, an `internal/item` service for CRUD + search against the existing `items` table, and resolver implementations wired into the router.

## Steps

### 1. Add gqlgen dependency
```bash
cd api && go get github.com/99designs/gqlgen@latest
```

### 2. Create gqlgen config + generate directive
- **`api/gqlgen.yml`** — schema-first config pointing at `graph/schema/*.graphql`, output to `graph/generated/` and `graph/model/`, resolvers in `graph/resolver/`. Map `DateTime` → `graphql.Time`, `JSON` → `graphql.Map`.
- **`api/graph/generate.go`** — `//go:generate go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate`

### 3. Create GraphQL SDL files in `api/graph/schema/`
- **`scalars.graphql`** — `DateTime`, `JSON`
- **`enums.graphql`** — `ItemType`, `ItemStatus`, `SensitivityLevel`, `ItemSortField`, `SortDirection`
- **`item.graphql`** — `Item` type, `SearchResult`, input types (`ItemFilter`, `ItemSort`, `BookmarkInput`, `NoteInput`, `UpdateItemInput`)
- **`schema.graphql`** — `Query` (item, items, search) + `Mutation` (captureBookmark, captureNote, updateItem, deleteItem, updateSensitivity, tagItem)

Schema follows the spec from the issue description exactly.

### 4. Run gqlgen codegen
`make api-generate` → generates `graph/generated/generated.go`, `graph/model/models_gen.go`, and resolver stubs.

### 5. Create `api/internal/item/` package

**`model.go`** — Domain types: `Item` struct (maps to DB columns), `Filter`, `Sort`, `SearchResult`, `BookmarkInput`, `NoteInput`, `UpdateInput`.

**`service.go`** — `Service` struct with `*pgxpool.Pool`. Methods:
- `GetByID(ctx, ownerID, itemID)` — SELECT with owner_id filter
- `List(ctx, ownerID, filter, limit, offset, sort)` — dynamic WHERE + ORDER BY + COUNT
- `Search(ctx, ownerID, query, filter, limit, threshold)` — FTS via `search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery`
- `CaptureBookmark(ctx, ownerID, input)` — INSERT with type=bookmark, status=pending
- `CaptureNote(ctx, ownerID, input)` — INSERT with type=text_note, status=ready
- `Update(ctx, ownerID, itemID, input)` — partial UPDATE (non-nil fields only)
- `Delete(ctx, ownerID, itemID)` — DELETE
- `UpdateSensitivity(ctx, ownerID, itemID, sensitivity)` — UPDATE sensitivity
- `Tag(ctx, ownerID, itemID, tags)` — UPDATE tags array

All SQL parameterized. All queries filter by `owner_id`. Use `RETURNING *` to avoid round-trips.

**`query.go`** — unexported query builder helper for dynamic filter → WHERE clause construction.

**`service_test.go`** — Unit tests for validation logic, query builder, enum mapping. Integration tests guarded by `testing.Short()`.

### 6. Implement resolvers in `api/graph/resolver/`

**`resolver.go`** — `Resolver` struct with `Items *item.Service`

**`helpers.go`** — `requireAuth(ctx)`, enum string↔GraphQL mappers, `toGraphQLItem()` domain→model mapper

**`schema.resolvers.go`** — Fill in generated stubs. Each resolver:
1. Calls `requireAuth(ctx)` for mutations (and item-access queries)
2. Maps GraphQL inputs → domain types
3. Calls `item.Service` method
4. Maps domain result → GraphQL model

### 7. Wire into router (`api/internal/router/router.go`)
- Add `Items *item.Service` to `Deps`
- Replace `placeholderHandler("graphql")` with real gqlgen `handler.New()`
- Add playground route if `GQLPlayground` is enabled
- Enable introspection if `GQLIntrospection` is enabled

### 8. Wire into main.go (`api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`)
- Create `item.NewService(pool)` after pool connection
- Pass to `router.Deps{..., Items: itemService}`

## Files to create
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `api/gqlgen.yml` | gqlgen config |
| `api/graph/generate.go` | go:generate directive |
| `api/graph/schema/scalars.graphql` | DateTime, JSON scalars |
| `api/graph/schema/enums.graphql` | All enum types |
| `api/graph/schema/item.graphql` | Item type, inputs, SearchResult |
| `api/graph/schema/schema.graphql` | Query + Mutation root types |
| `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` | Resolver struct |
| `api/graph/resolver/helpers.go` | Auth check, mappers |
| `api/internal/item/model.go` | Domain types |
| `api/internal/item/service.go` | CRUD + search service |
| `api/internal/item/query.go` | Dynamic query builder |
| `api/internal/item/service_test.go` | Unit tests |

## Files to modify
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `api/go.mod` | Add gqlgen dependency |
| `api/internal/router/router.go` | Wire gqlgen handler, add Items to Deps |
| `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go` | Create item service, pass to router |

## Generated files (never hand-edit)
- `api/graph/generated/generated.go`
- `api/graph/model/models_gen.go`
- `api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go` (stubs generated, then filled in)

## Key existing code to reuse
- `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` → `api/internal/auth/context.go:13`
- `auth.Claims.UserID` → `api/internal/auth/auth.go`
- `router.Deps` → `api/internal/router/router.go:21`
- `config.GQLPlayground`, `config.GQLIntrospection` → `api/internal/config/config.go:37-38`

## Verification
```bash
make api-generate          # gqlgen codegen succeeds
make api-test-unit         # all tests pass
go vet ./...               # no issues (fallback if golangci-lint unavailable)
make api-build             # all binaries compile
```

# Plan: GraphQL Schema + Resolvers (flt-it8.4)

## Context

The Flotsam API has a working HTTP server with auth middleware, database connection, and a placeholder `/graphql` endpoint returning 501. This plan adds the real GraphQL layer: SDL schema, gqlgen codegen, an `internal/item` service for CRUD + search against the existing `items` table, and resolver implementations wired into the router.

## Steps

### 1. Add gqlgen dependency
```bash
cd api && go get github.com/99designs/gqlgen@latest
```

### 2. Create gqlgen config + generate directive
- **`api/gqlgen.yml`** — schema-first config pointing at `graph/schema/*.graphql`, output to `graph/generated/` and `graph/model/`, resolvers in `graph/resolver/`. Map `DateTime` → `graphql.Time`, `JSON` → `graphql.Map`.
- **`api/graph/generate.go`** — `//go:generate go run github.com/99designs/gqlgen generate`

### 3. Create GraphQL SDL files in `api/graph/schema/`
- **`scalars.graphql`** — `DateTime`, `JSON`
- **`enums.graphql`** — `ItemType`, `ItemStatus`, `SensitivityLevel`, `ItemSortField`, `SortDirection`
- **`item.graphql`** — `Item` type, `SearchResult`, input types (`ItemFilter`, `ItemSort`, `BookmarkInput`, `NoteInput`, `UpdateItemInput`)
- **`schema.graphql`** — `Query` (item, items, search) + `Mutation` (captureBookmark, captureNote, updateItem, deleteItem, updateSensitivity, tagItem)

Schema follows the spec from the issue description exactly.

### 4. Run gqlgen codegen
`make api-generate` → generates `graph/generated/generated.go`, `graph/model/models_gen.go`, and resolver stubs.

### 5. Create `api/internal/item/` package

**`model.go`** — Domain types: `Item` struct (maps to DB columns), `Filter`, `Sort`, `SearchResult`, `BookmarkInput`, `NoteInput`, `UpdateInput`.

**`service.go`** — `Service` struct with `*pgxpool.Pool`. Methods:
- `GetByID(ctx, ownerID, itemID)` — SELECT with owner_id filter
- `List(ctx, ownerID, filter, limit, offset, sort)` — dynamic WHERE + ORDER BY + COUNT
- `Search(ctx, ownerID, query, filter, limit, threshold)` — FTS via `search_vector @@ plainto_tsquery`
- `CaptureBookmark(ctx, ownerID, input)` — INSERT with type=bookmark, status=pending
- `CaptureNote(ctx, ownerID, input)` — INSERT with type=text_note, status=ready
- `Update(ctx, ownerID, itemID, input)` — partial UPDATE (non-nil fields only)
- `Delete(ctx, ownerID, itemID)` — DELETE
- `UpdateSensitivity(ctx, ownerID, itemID, sensitivity)` — UPDATE sensitivity
- `Tag(ctx, ownerID, itemID, tags)` — UPDATE tags array

All SQL parameterized. All queries filter by `owner_id`. Use `RETURNING *` to avoid round-trips.

**`query.go`** — unexported query builder helper for dynamic filter → WHERE clause construction.

**`service_test.go`** — Unit tests for validation logic, query builder, enum mapping. Integration tests guarded by `testing.Short()`.

### 6. Implement resolvers in `api/graph/resolver/`

**`resolver.go`** — `Resolver` struct with `Items *item.Service`

**`helpers.go`** — `requireAuth(ctx)`, enum string↔GraphQL mappers, `toGraphQLItem()` domain→model mapper

**`schema.resolvers.go`** — Fill in generated stubs. Each resolver:
1. Calls `requireAuth(ctx)` for mutations (and item-access queries)
2. Maps GraphQL inputs → domain types
3. Calls `item.Service` method
4. Maps domain result → GraphQL model

### 7. Wire into router (`api/internal/router/router.go`)
- Add `Items *item.Service` to `Deps`
- Replace `placeholderHandler("graphql")` with real gqlgen `handler.New()`
- Add playground route if `GQLPlayground` is enabled
- Enable introspection if `GQLIntrospection` is enabled

### 8. Wire into main.go (`api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go`)
- Create `item.NewService(pool)` after pool connection
- Pass to `router.Deps{..., Items: itemService}`

## Files to create
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `api/gqlgen.yml` | gqlgen config |
| `api/graph/generate.go` | go:generate directive |
| `api/graph/schema/scalars.graphql` | DateTime, JSON scalars |
| `api/graph/schema/enums.graphql` | All enum types |
| `api/graph/schema/item.graphql` | Item type, inputs, SearchResult |
| `api/graph/schema/schema.graphql` | Query + Mutation root types |
| `api/graph/resolver/resolver.go` | Resolver struct |
| `api/graph/resolver/helpers.go` | Auth check, mappers |
| `api/internal/item/model.go` | Domain types |
| `api/internal/item/service.go` | CRUD + search service |
| `api/internal/item/query.go` | Dynamic query builder |
| `api/internal/item/service_test.go` | Unit tests |

## Files to modify
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `api/go.mod` | Add gqlgen dependency |
| `api/internal/router/router.go` | Wire gqlgen handler, add Items to Deps |
| `api/cmd/flotsamd/main.go` | Create item service, pass to router |

## Generated files (never hand-edit)
- `api/graph/generated/generated.go`
- `api/graph/model/models_gen.go`
- `api/graph/resolver/schema.resolvers.go` (stubs generated, then filled in)

## Key existing code to reuse
- `auth.GetClaims(ctx)` → `api/internal/auth/context.go:13`
- `auth.Claims.UserID` → `api/internal/auth/auth.go`
- `router.Deps` → `api/internal/router/router.go:21`
- `config.GQLPlayground`, `config.GQLIntrospection` → `api/internal/config/config.go:37-38`

## Verification
```bash
make api-generate          # gqlgen codegen succeeds
make api-test-unit         # all tests pass
go vet ./...               # no issues (fallback if golangci-lint unavailable)
make api-build             # all binaries compile
```
